task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the correct target website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A browser, scraping tool, or script configuration showing 'https://books.toscrape.com/' as the target URL.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configured extraction for the three required <head> tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code (e.g., Python/BeautifulSoup) or a scraping tool interface showing logic/selectors targeting the Viewport meta tag, Favicon link tag, and Charset/Content-Type meta tag.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent crawled at least 20 URLs from the site.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal log, crawler progress bar, or data table showing that 20 or more distinct URLs from books.toscrape.com were processed.

task_completion.description: Verify the extracted data was saved to the exact requested file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer, terminal command (e.g., ls), or script showing the creation of 'head_tags_audit.csv' specifically in the '~/Documents/SEO/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the extracted CSV contains the correct values for the head tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A preview of the CSV data or script output showing the expected values: 'width=device-width' (Viewport), 'static/oscar/favicon.ico' or containing 'icon' (Favicon), and 'UTF-8' or containing 'utf' (Charset).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a genuine scraping method (scripting, scraping software, or browser extension) rather than manually typing the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a script (e.g., Python), scraping tool UI, or terminal execution logs rather than just a text editor with manually typed CSV data.

integrity.description: Verify the 20+ URLs crawled are actual pages from the target site.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output data shows real paths from the site (e.g., /catalogue/...) rather than dummy URLs or the exact same URL copy-pasted 20 times.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just hardcode the expected snippets directly into a CSV without actually fetching them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code or tool configuration should show dynamic extraction (e.g., finding the tag and getting its attribute) rather than hardcoded strings like 'width=device-width' being written directly to the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://books.toscrape.com/. Expected extraction values: Viewport content must be 'width=device-width', Favicon href must be 'static/oscar/favicon.ico' (or contain 'icon'), and Charset/Content-Type must be 'UTF-8' (or contain 'utf'). The site has 50 pagination pages and 1000 product URLs, making a 20+ URL crawl highly feasible. The final export must be located exactly at '~/Documents/SEO/exports/head_tags_audit.csv'.
